nativeRange.habitat: Reef slopes of clear to moderately turbid water, shallow to mid-depths; large colonies typically occur in shaded areas such as under overhangs

diet.notes: Primarily photosynthetic via zooxanthellae. Supplemental target-feeding of small meaty foods such as Mysis shrimp, brine shrimp, or coral-specific foods weekly is generally recommended to support growth and polyp extension, though the coral can survive on light alone in well-established reef systems.

avoidWith: aggressive LPS corals with long sweeper tentacles

summary: Plesiastrea versipora is a hardy encrusting/massive LPS coral from the Indo-Pacific, often mistaken for Cyphastrea. Generally considered beginner-friendly with moderate lighting and flow tolerance.
careNotes:
Plesiastrea versipora (sometimes listed simply as Plesiastrea sp. in the trade) is the sole widely kept species in the monotypic family Plesiastreidae. It is frequently confused with Cyphastrea species in the aquarium hobby due to its similarly small, spaced corallites and encrusting growth form, though Plesiastrea tends to have slightly more fleshy polyps and greater spacing between corallites. The genus Plesiastrea is distinct from former Faviidae placement and is now recognized in its own family.

Growth form: Encrusting to submassive; can become significantly massive in the wild (documented colonies reaching up to 1 m in thickness in high-latitude habitats). In captivity, colonies generally remain 10–40 cm, spreading across rockwork and substrate rather than building upward.

Lighting: Generally recommended at moderate intensity (roughly 75–200 PAR). New specimens are best introduced at lower light and gradually acclimated. Unlike SPS corals, Plesiastrea tends to be tolerant of a wide range of light intensities and can thrive under overhangs or in shaded reef zones.

Flow: Moderate, indirect water movement is generally preferred. Direct laminar flow should be avoided as it can irritate tissue.

Placement: Flexible — the coral encrusts onto whatever surface it contacts, making it suitable for most tank positions. It can be placed anywhere in the aquarium and will spread laterally.

Aggression: Generally considered low-aggression; does not appear to extend long sweeper tentacles like Favites or Goniastrea. Maintain standard spacing of 2–3 cm from neighbors as a precaution.

Feeding: Plesiastrea versipora is a zooxanthellate coral that derives most energy from photosynthesis. Weekly supplemental target-feeding with small meaty foods (Mysis, Cyclops, coral amino acids) has been reported to support coloration and growth. Extended daytime tentacle activity (documented in wild specimens) suggests it is capable of capturing small food particles.

Water chemistry: Follows standard reef parameters — alkalinity 8–9 dKH, calcium 400–450 ppm, magnesium 1250–1400 ppm, salinity SG 1.025–1.026, temperature 24–28°C, pH 8.0–8.4. It is generally considered more tolerant of slightly variable conditions than SPS corals, making it well-suited to beginner reef systems.

Aquacultured availability: Multiple coral vendors (e.g., World Wide Corals) offer aquacultured Plesiastrea frags, including color morphs marketed as 'Neon Plesiastrea' and 'Mint Chip Plesiastrea', which tend to be bright green. Wild-collected specimens may occasionally appear.

Note: P. versipora thrives across both tropical reef and higher-latitude (temperate/subtropical) marine environments in the wild — it is one of the more eurythermal reef corals described. Aquarium care should target tropical reef parameters as standard.
breedingNotes: Fragging via bone cutters or a bandsaw is the primary propagation method in captivity. Frags encrust readily onto frag plugs or rubble. Sexual reproduction occurs via broadcast spawning in the wild; captive sexual breeding is not practiced in the hobby.

primary.name: World Wide Corals – Plesiastrea product listings
primary.notes: Product listings for WWC Mint Chip Plesiastrea and WWC Neon Plesiastrea confirmed care level Easy, lighting Medium, flow Medium. Full numeric water parameters not provided on product pages.

additional.name: WoRMS – Plesiastrea versipora (AphiaID 207494)
additional.notes: Used for taxonomy (Family Plesiastreidae, Order Scleractinia), native range (Indo-Pacific, 64 documented distribution records), and habitat description. Confirmed: reef slopes shallow to mid-depths, shaded overhangs; colonies can reach 3 m in high-latitude habitats; zooxanthellate; daytime tentacle extension noted.

additional.name: Wikipedia – Plesiastrea versipora
additional.notes: Used for growth form confirmation (encrusting/massive, up to 1 m thickness), long-lived colony documentation, and range (Indian and Pacific Oceans). Not used for husbandry parameters.

additional.name: ReefBuilders – Plesiastrea identification and taxonomy
additional.notes: Confirmed flat-to-semi-lobed growth form, small corallites, frequent confusion with Cyphastrea, aquacultured availability, Indo-Pacific distribution. Husbandry parameters not covered.

additional.name: ReefChasers – Cyphastrea Coral Care Guide (cross-reference for closely related faviid LPS)
additional.notes: Used as secondary cross-reference for water parameters of a closely related faviid LPS coral (Cyphastrea, formerly in same family Faviidae). Confirmed: temperature 77–84°F (25–29°C), pH 8.0–8.4, alkalinity 8–9 dKH, calcium 400–450 ppm, lighting 100–200 PAR, flow moderate, care beginner. Values consistent with Favites (another faviid) guide from same source.
